new_value:
{"acceptance_criteria":"Decision record defines evidence tiers, freezes pack_id/usage_id semantics, documents advisory-only launch, and specifies which signals may or may not drive ranking, promotion, demotion, or deletion.","description":"Background: UMS already has replay-gated evaluation, explainability, and lineage, but attribution will be risky unless the project freezes what counts as evidence and what automation is allowed.

Scope:
- Define canonical pack_id, usage_id, and outcome-linkage semantics.
- Define evidence tiers: observational, deterministic replay/ablation, and randomized canary.
- Define which tiers may influence ranking, promotion, demotion, and deletion.
- Freeze an advisory-only launch posture with default-off and kill-switch language.

Non-goals:
- No scoring implementation in this issue.
- No direct mutation authority for attribution.

Parallelization note: This issue is intentionally self-contained and should produce the contract and safety envelope that other tasks can target without redesigning the roadmap.","title":"[P4-E6] Freeze tiered credit-assignment contract and safety envelope"}

Non-goals:
- No scoring implementation in this issue.
- No direct mutation authority for attribution.

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.11","title":"[P4-E11] Define privacy, retention, and tenant-isolation rules for usage traces","description":"Background: Fine-grained usage tracing creates a privacy and governance burden that must be constrained before broad rollout.

Scope:
- Define the minimum trace fields required for attribution.
- Specify hashing or pseudonymization rules, retention windows, and same-tenant-only join rules.
- Define per-space or per-tenant disable and kill-switch behavior.
- Document operator expectations for auditability and deletion or expiration.

Non-goals:
- No scorer implementation in this issue.
- No dashboard work in this issue.

Parallelization note: This is a policy and governance slice and should not be blocked on scorer implementation details.","acceptance_criteria":"Policy or decision record defines minimal trace fields, pseudonymization rules, retention bounds, same-tenant join rules, and disable or kill-switch requirements for attribution traces.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"decision","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:24Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:24Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Drafted proposed ADR in docs/adr/0010-usage-trace-privacy-retention-tenancy.md and added runbook reference in docs/runbooks/phase1-phase2-backend-delivery-runbook.md."}

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.7","title":"[P4-E7] Add deterministic pack_id and usage_id emission to context surfaces","description":"Background: Credit assignment needs deterministic trace identifiers at context-generation time so downstream outcomes can reference what was actually surfaced.

Scope:
- Extend context surfaces to emit stable pack_id and per-item usage_id values.
- Thread the identifiers through API, CLI, and MCP contracts without leaking tenant boundaries.
- Add deterministic replay tests to ensure repeated runs emit the same ids for the same inputs.
- Keep payload growth explicitly bounded and documented.

Non-goals:
- No outcome persistence in this issue.
- No scoring or ranking logic in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue owns context-side identifiers only; it should not absorb outcome linkage, scoring, or dashboard work.","acceptance_criteria":"Context responses emit stable pack_id and usage_id fields across supported surfaces, replay tests prove determinism, and payload budgets remain bounded or explicitly documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:24:43Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T11:24:43Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented deterministic packId/usageId emission in apps/api/src/core.ts and added contract coverage in apps/api/test/core.test.ts plus apps/api/test/server.test.ts."}

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.8","title":"[P4-E8] Persist outcome linkage for traced memory usage","description":"Background: Attribution is impossible if outcomes cannot point back to the traced memory pack and the items that were actually available to the agent.

Scope:
- Extend outcome persistence contracts to accept pack_id, referenced usage_ids, and bounded artifact, test, and tool references.
- Preserve idempotent replay-safe writes and same-tenant lineage constraints.
- Define provisional vs settled outcome timestamps if the model needs delayed resolution.
- Add tests for replay safety, tenant isolation, and lineage completeness.

Non-goals:
- No attribution scorer in this issue.
- No UI or reporting work in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue is outcome-side only; it should not change context ranking or scoring behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"Outcome contracts store pack_id and usage_id lineage with replay-safe same-tenant persistence, delayed-outcome semantics are documented if needed, and tests cover replay and isolation guarantees.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:24:53Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T11:24:53Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Extended outcome linkage with packId, usedUsageIds, bounded tool/test/artifact refs, and provisional/settled timestamps in apps/api/src/core.ts; added replay/isolation coverage in apps/api/test/core.test.ts."}

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.9","title":"[P4-E9] Build shadow-only attribution scorer with uncertainty fields","description":"Background: The safest first version of attribution is shadow-only: compute estimated lift and harm, but do not let it directly mutate active memory.

Scope:
- Implement a shadow attribution scorer that consumes traced context and outcome data.
- Output uncertainty-first fields such as effect size, confidence, sample size, support thresholds, and reason codes.
- Return low-confidence or no-op results for sparse or ambiguous data instead of forcing a score.
- Keep the scorer detached from automatic promote, demote, or delete paths.

Non-goals:
- No direct ranking mutation in this issue.
- No calibration corpus in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue owns the scorer contract and implementation only; policy, dashboards, and integration stay elsewhere.","acceptance_criteria":"Scorer computes estimated helpful or harmful contribution with explicit confidence and sample-size fields, degrades gracefully on sparse data, and has no direct path to automatic memory mutation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:01Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:01Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.10","title":"[P4-E10] Add attribution calibration suite with negative controls","description":"Background: Attribution systems fail when they confidently reward noise, miss synergy, or cannot distinguish useful signal from irrelevant context.

Scope:
- Build a deterministic calibration suite with negative controls, irrelevant-memory cases, paired-synergy cases, delayed outcomes, and safety-regression cases.
- Add pass/fail thresholds for false positives, confidence inflation, and unstable scoring under replay.
- Ensure the suite is CI-friendly and bounded.

Non-goals:
- No production dashboarding in this issue.
- No ranking integration in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue owns validation of the scorer, not the scorer itself or contract plumbing.","acceptance_criteria":"Deterministic evaluation cases cover irrelevant memories, synergy pairs, delayed outcomes, and safety regressions, with explicit thresholds that catch noisy or overconfident attribution.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:17Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:17Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.12","title":"[P4-E12] Publish attribution report with uncertainty-first advisory UX","description":"Background: Operators need an uncertainty-first view of attribution data before the project should trust it in any automated path.

Scope:
- Publish a report or dashboard view for attributed lift or harm, confidence, sample size, and support counts.
- Clearly label all outputs as advisory-only while the system is in shadow mode.
- Surface top helpful and harmful memories with links back to lineage and evaluation artifacts.
- Keep the surface read-only.

Non-goals:
- No mutation controls in this issue.
- No policy definition in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue is reporting-only and should consume existing scorer outputs rather than reshaping them.","acceptance_criteria":"A read-only report or dashboard shows attributed lift or harm alongside confidence and support counts, marks all output advisory-only, and links back to lineage and evaluation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:33Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:33Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.13","title":"[P4-E13] Gate attribution to advisory-only ranking nudges behind kill switch","description":"Background: If attribution is ever allowed to influence runtime behavior, it should start with tightly bounded ranking nudges under explicit kill-switch control, never direct memory mutation.

Scope:
- Define and implement an advisory-only integration path from attribution outputs into retrieval ranking heuristics.
- Require per-space or per-tenant kill switches and easy rollback.
- Prevent attribution from directly promoting, demoting, or deleting memory.
- Add monitoring to compare ranked-with-attribution vs baseline behavior.

Non-goals:
- No direct promote or demote authority.
- No irreversible memory changes.

Parallelization note: This issue is the only runtime integration slice and must stay narrowly bounded to ranking nudges and rollback controls.","acceptance_criteria":"Attribution may only influence bounded ranking heuristics behind per-space kill switches, monitoring compares attributed vs baseline behavior, and there is no direct path to promote, demote, or delete memory.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:40Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:40Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented read-only  scoring in apps/api/src/core.ts with uncertainty-first fields, replay lineage, advisory-only guarantees, and deterministic tests in apps/api/test/core.test.ts."}

- Clearly label all outputs as advisory-only while the system is in shadow mode.
- Surface top helpful and harmful memories with links back to lineage and evaluation artifacts.
- Keep the surface read-only.

Non-goals:
- No mutation controls in this issue.
- No policy definition in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue is reporting-only and should consume existing scorer outputs rather than reshaping them.","acceptance_criteria":"A read-only report or dashboard shows attributed lift or harm alongside confidence and support counts, marks all output advisory-only, and links back to lineage and evaluation artifacts.","notes":"Added read-only  output in apps/api/src/core.ts with helpful/harmful/pending sections plus lineage refs; covered in apps/api/test/core.test.ts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:33Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T17:00:07Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T17:00:07Z","close_reason":"Completed"}

{"id":"ums-memory-jny.13","title":"[P4-E13] Gate attribution to advisory-only ranking nudges behind kill switch","description":"Background: If attribution is ever allowed to influence runtime behavior, it should start with tightly bounded ranking nudges under explicit kill-switch control, never direct memory mutation.

Scope:
- Define and implement an advisory-only integration path from attribution outputs into retrieval ranking heuristics.
- Require per-space or per-tenant kill switches and easy rollback.
- Prevent attribution from directly promoting, demoting, or deleting memory.
- Add monitoring to compare ranked-with-attribution vs baseline behavior.

Non-goals:
- No direct promote or demote authority.
- No irreversible memory changes.

Parallelization note: This issue is the only runtime integration slice and must stay narrowly bounded to ranking nudges and rollback controls.","acceptance_criteria":"Attribution may only influence bounded ranking heuristics behind per-space kill switches, monitoring compares attributed vs baseline behavior, and there is no direct path to promote, demote, or delete memory.","notes":"Added per-space  kill switch and bounded advisory ranking nudges in context responses with baseline-vs-ranked observability; covered in apps/api/test/core.test.ts and apps/api/test/server.test.ts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T11:25:40Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T17:00:08Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T17:00:08Z","close_reason":"Completed"}

new_value: {"acceptance_criteria":"ums install registers the daemon with supported OS supervisors, is idempotent on re-run, and ums uninstall cleanly removes service state without corrupting user memory."}
old_value:
{"id":"ums-memory-c0j.2","title":"[P0-D2] Implement one-command installer and service supervisor registration","description":"Background: One-command install is core to the product shape. The user should not manually wire service managers or bootstrap files.

Scope:
- Implement `ums install` / `ums uninstall` bootstrap behavior.
- Register the daemon with the host supervisor (`launchd`, `systemd`, Windows service/task runner) with safe defaults.
- Add deterministic upgrade/reinstall handling, including idempotent re-runs.
- Persist install metadata and service registration state for `ums status` / `ums doctor`.

Non-goals:
- No ingestion logic beyond bootstrap.
- No managed sync/login semantics in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue is OS/service bootstrap only; runtime collector, CLI command UX, and agent instructions should remain separate.","acceptance_criteria":" registers the daemon with supported OS supervisors, is idempotent on re-run, and  cleanly removes service state without corrupting user memory.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:16:26Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:16:26Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-c0j.1","title":"[P0-D1] Freeze daemon-first CLI contract and solo-vs-managed parity","description":"Background: UMS should behave like one product for solo and company users. The external surface must be CLI-first and daemon-first, while remote MCP stays deferred.

Scope:
- Freeze the CLI command surface for install, lifecycle control, sync, doctor, export, and memory management.
- Freeze the daemon-first runtime model and same-external-contract rule for solo and managed modes.
- Define what is automatic versus what requires user consent (sources, login, retention/policy overrides).
- Explicitly defer remote MCP gateway work until the CLI runtime reaches parity and stability.

Non-goals:
- No installer implementation in this issue.
- No adapter implementation in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue should establish the contract and lifecycle vocabulary so installer, daemon, CLI, skill, and sync work can proceed without reopening product semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"Decision record defines the daemon-first lifecycle, CLI command families, same external behavior for solo/managed users, and explicitly defers remote MCP until later.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"decision","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:16:26Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:16:26Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-c0j.13","title":"[P0-D13] Implement JSONC daemon config schema and legacy-session migration","description":"Background: ADR-0011 freezes JSONC as the default declarative daemon config format and replaces the flat legacy account-session model as the primary configuration surface.

Scope:
- Define the JSONC schema for accounts, memories, routes, defaults, and policy controls.
- Implement strict validation and canonical normalization rules.
- Add migration/import from legacy account-session.json into the new config/state layout.
- Publish config validate/doctor expectations and schema export path.

Non-goals:
- No keychain integration in this issue.
- No route-resolution engine implementation in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue freezes the config contract so auth, CLI registry, routing, and journal work can proceed without reopening shape decisions.","acceptance_criteria":"A documented schema and migration contract exists for JSONC daemon config, legacy session import behavior is defined, and validation/normalization rules are explicit.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:46:53Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:46:53Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-c0j.15","title":"[P0-D15] Implement named account, memory, and route registry CLI","description":"Background: The daemon config model is built around named accounts, memories, and routes instead of a single login/connection pair. Users and agents need a CLI-first way to create and inspect those entities.

Scope:
- Add CLI commands for account, memory, and route creation/list/update/remove flows.
- Validate cross-references (memory -> account, route -> memory).
- Add route explain/test surfaces so agents and users can inspect deterministic routing decisions.
- Keep the same external contract for solo and managed users.

Non-goals:
- No secure credential storage implementation in this issue.
- No delivery journal implementation in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue depends on the schema contract and existing daemon control-plane command families, but is otherwise independent of auth internals.","acceptance_criteria":"CLI can create and inspect accounts, memories, and routes with strict validation and deterministic explain/test output for routing decisions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:46:54Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:46:54Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-c0j.17","title":"[P0-D17] Implement deterministic project/path routing and ambiguity policy","description":"Background: Users need rules like “everything under this folder goes to memory X, everything else to Y”. ADR-0011 requires deterministic routing precedence and explicit ambiguity handling.

Scope:
- Resolve path/repo/workspace metadata into named memory targets using deterministic precedence.
- Implement explicit ambiguity policy behavior (`review`, `default`, `drop`).
- Add provenance/explain output for “why did this event route to memory X?”.
- Ensure routing decisions preserve solo/managed parity and fail safe on missing metadata.

Non-goals:
- No keychain/login implementation in this issue.
- No delivery journal implementation in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue depends on config schema, collector metadata, and CLI route registry, but can proceed independently of managed auth internals.","acceptance_criteria":"Project/path routing rules produce deterministic memory targets, ambiguity handling is explicit, and explain output can show why a given event or path resolved to a specific memory.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:46:55Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:46:55Z"}

{"id":"ums-memory-3qg","title":"[P0-D16A] Hard-cut replace legacy account-session sync state with config-backed daemon runtime","description":"Background: apps/ums/src/account-sync.ts still drives login/connect/sync/status through a plaintext single-account account-session.json prototype. Pre-release we want a hard breaking cutover to the JSONC config/runtime-state model from ADR-0011.\\n\\nScope:\\n- Remove CLI dependence on legacy account-session.json for sync/status flows.\\n- Introduce config-backed daemon runtime state under ~/.ums/state with local collection and per-memory delivery bookkeeping.\\n- Route collected events through named memories using deterministic route resolution.\\n- Preserve CLI-first machine-readable outputs for sync/status while failing closed on unsupported managed auth.\\n\\nNon-goals:\\n- No secure keychain login implementation in this issue.\\n- No OS service installer work in this issue.\\n\\nAcceptance:\\n- ums sync/status use config.jsonc + state root instead of account-session.json.\\n- local memories ingest end-to-end through routed buckets.\\n- HTTP memories fail explicitly until keychain/auth lands.\\n- legacy login/connect account-session path is removed from the main sync lifecycle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"syreanis+1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T19:47:01Z","created_by":"Petr Glaser","updated_at":"2026-03-06T19:47:01Z"}
